channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's promised 'Liberation Day' of tariffs is coming. Here's what it could mean for you
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says Wednesday will be “Liberation Day" — a moment when he plans to roll out a set of tariffs that he promises will free the United States from foreign goods. The details of Trump's next round of import taxes are still sketchy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump roars down multiple paths of retribution as he vowed. Some targets yield while others fight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The executive order directed at one of the country's most prestigious law firms followed a well-worn playbook as President Donald Trump roared down the road to retribution.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Betts, top overall seed UCLA look for March Madness success in Elite Eight meeting with LSU
item.description: With back-to-back games of at least 30 points and 10 or more rebounds, UCLA center Lauren Betts certainly appears unstoppable. But, LSU has thwarted the Bruins before — in the Sweet 16 of last years women’s NCAA Tournament. They'll get a rematch on Sunday, this time in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Plane crash near Minneapolis sends home up in flames with no survivors on board
item.description: Dramatic video captured the aftermath of a home being engulfed in flames near Minneapolis after a small plane crashed into the home on Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, ABC 4

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds have protested billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Close call reported between Delta flight and Air Force jet near Reagan National
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight had a reported close call Friday with a U.S. Air Force jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Jay Blackman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta plane, Air Force jet nearly crash in 'loss of separation' during Arlington National Cemetery flyover
item.description: A Delta plane carrying 131 passengers nearly collided with an Air Force jet in a 'loss of separation' Friday leading up to a flyover at Arlington National Cemetery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Pizza Kitchen celebrates 40 years with throwback menu and prizes
item.description: California Pizza Kitchen is celebrating its 40th anniversary with a series of promotions, returning menu items and a collaboration with actor and former employee actress Busy Philipps.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter detained by feds
item.description: A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: Prosecutors in the killings of 4 Idaho students seek to use man's crime scene paper as evidence
item.description: Prosecutors in the case of a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students are seeking to use as evidence a college paper he wrote that shows his knowledge of crime scenes, according to a court filing this week Bryan Kohberger, 30, is accused in the stabbing deaths of Ethan Chapin
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bride-to-be brutally attacked during bachelorette trip in Dallas
item.description: An Oklahoma bride-to-be's bachelorette trip turned into a nightmare and something she will never forget, all for the wrong reasons.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Atlantic posts more texts from Signal chat group
item.description: The Trump administration is in damage control mode, after the Atlantic released more text messages detailing attack plans from a group chat which included national security officials.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, Fox 5

item.title: New Social Security clawback policy starts this week: What to know
item.description: A major change in how the Social Security Administration (SSA) treats its overpayments is set to kick in Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked agents handcuffing her
item.description: Rumeysa Ozturk's whereabouts are unknown and no known charges have been filed against her, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court won't halt order barring Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NATO issues new statement on fate of 4 missing Fort Stewart soldiers
item.description: WIESBADEN, Germany (WSAV) — The NATO Acting spokesperson said that the Secretary General was referring to news articles and did not confirm the death of four U.S. Army 3rd Infantry Division soldiers reported missing in Lithuania Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4
